objective: Implement the fourth #298 child slice: discover, classify, and registry-cover EmailAssistant without downstream repository edits.
scope_boundary: Inspect EmailAssistant through a temporary read-only checkout and GitHub API/CLI.
Document stack, default branch, privacy/data sensitivity, CI state, governance-doc state, branch/ruleset evidence, and downstream blockers.
Add dispatcher registry coverage for EmailAssistant with adoption-blocked governance status and explicit classification.
Open repo-local EmailAssistant issue(s) before any downstream source or governance-file edits.
Add issue-backed planning, exception, cross-review, execution-scope, backlog/progress, and validation evidence for #312.
out_of_scope: Adding graphify/wiki artifacts for EmailAssistant before repo-local governance bootstrap exists.
Reconciling README/STANDARDS fleet registry surfaces, which remains #313.
Claiming final org-wide governance e2e completion.
research_summary: EmailAssistant is an active private repository on default branch main. It is a Python 3.11 Windows-oriented municipal email assistant with Flask review UI, Outlook/EWS, Microsoft Graph, SMTP, Anthropic, installer, and local JSON persistence. It has no GitHub workflows, no CODEOWNERS, no dependabot, no CLAUDE/AGENTS/CODEX front-door files, and no PROGRESS/FEATURE_REGISTRY/DATA_DICTIONARY/SERVICE_REGISTRY/FAIL_FAST_LOG docs. Org branch rulesets apply, but repo branch protection does not expose required checks. Data sensitivity is high because the app handles municipal email, credentials, PIP/personnel context, drafts, commitments, logs, and style profiles. Dispatcher-side registry coverage is added as adoption_blocked; repo-local bootstrap is tracked by EmailAssistant#1.

sprints.name: Read-Only Discovery
sprints.goal: Establish EmailAssistant repo state, stack, governance surface, and data sensitivity without downstream edits.
tasks: Inspect GitHub metadata, rulesets, branch protection, workflows, and default branch.
Clone EmailAssistant to a temporary inspection directory.
Inspect repo front-door files, stack files, config template, and sensitive runtime surfaces.
Spawn a read-only explorer for independent discovery review.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: EmailAssistant checkout/inspection evidence exists.
Final AC: stack, default branch, privacy/data sensitivity, CI, and governance-doc state are documented.
Final AC: no downstream repo files are edited.

sprints.name: Classification And Blocker
sprints.goal: Represent EmailAssistant in governance while preserving repo-local adoption as issue-backed work.
tasks: Classify governance tier, security tier, lifecycle status, and governance status.
Open EmailAssistant#1 for downstream bootstrap before future repo writes.
Add adoption-blocked registry coverage with subsystem flags disabled until downstream bootstrap resolves.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: governance/security tier classification is recorded.
Final AC: registry coverage is added or a linked blocker issue has owner/rationale/review date.
Final AC: downstream remediation is split into repo-local issue-backed work.

sprints.name: Final E2E Proof
sprints.goal: Prove the #312 discovery/classification closes active-repo inventory drift without overstating governance adoption.
tasks: Run live org inventory drift check in blocking mode.
Run structured plan, execution-scope, backlog sync, Local CI Gate, py_compile, and diff hygiene checks.
Run alternate/specialist review before PR publication.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: live org inventory blocking mode no longer reports EmailAssistant as missing.
Final AC: registry validation passes with EmailAssistant adoption_blocked.
Final AC: Local CI Gate passes for changed paths.
Final AC: #312 remains open unless PR and e2e evidence are linked.
Final AC: parent #298 remains open until #313 and #314 complete.

specialist_reviews.focus: Repo state, workflows, rulesets, front-door governance files, sensitivity, and downstream follow-up needs.
specialist_reviews.summary: Read-only explorer confirmed EmailAssistant is active/private, has no workflows or repo-local governance docs, has org rulesets but no visible required checks, and should be classified full/full-pentagi with repo-local bootstrap before downstream writes.

alternate_model_review.summary: Subagent discovery accepted adoption-blocked registry coverage and repo-local downstream bootstrap as the appropriate boundary for #312.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #312 may inspect EmailAssistant, add dispatcher registry/planning/validation artifacts, and open repo-local EmailAssistant issue(s). It may not edit EmailAssistant source or governance files.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Complete adoption-blocked registry coverage, validation evidence, and PR for #312.

material_deviation_rules: If EmailAssistant source or governance-file edits are needed, stop and move that work to EmailAssistant#1 or another downstream issue-backed scope.
If graph/wiki coverage is required, keep it disabled until downstream bootstrap creates a stable governed surface or a separate issue authorizes graph artifact generation.
If live inventory still reports EmailAssistant missing after registry coverage, do not publish.
If #313 surface reconciliation is needed, stop and leave it to #313.
If final org-wide e2e closure is needed, stop and leave it to #314.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules, no HITL, e2e testing as final AC
